full_text:
Reviewer comment requirements

Text edits

Figure edits

Reviewer 1:

verification of the final conclusion that cancers positive for pRPA32 in cancer cells have PD-1 CD8-positive T cells in the tumor vicinity and show high efficacy of ATR inhibitors is insufficient.

-> is there more we can extract from the data (Ziqi)? No.

->>Tone down final statements in text and highlighting etc.?

We have done spatial statistics, even though the sample amount is small, the spatial data still is significant. Added to rebuttal letter

Paper co-authed by Anniina - replication stress related to ATRi response (https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-021-25904-w) added

The study demonstrates using iPDCs experiments that the efficacy of PD-1 inhibitors is high in HRD-positive cancers and low in HRP cancers. However, pRPA32, which is typically a replication stress marker, is higher in high-grade serous cancers with CCNE1 amplification (which accounts for 40% of HRP cancers) than in HRD ovarian cancers.

-> CCNE1 data from WES? Seek for correlations with response

- reviewer confusing 1st part with PD-1 inhibitor, and the 2nd part that uses the pRPA32 as a marker for ATRi response rebuttal

-PD-1 more efficient for HRD - correlates with TOPACIO. In TOPACIO, signature 3 (HRD) was sign assoc. with PARP+PD1 therapy response - now confirmed in iPDC. However we now see that pRPA32-marked replication stress is sign assoc. with ATRi response and combs with immunotherapies. Not limited to CCNE1amp only, also other genomic events lead to repl. Stress -> atri response. Clarify in discussion rebuttal + text

- mention that only a subset of repl. stress cases have CCNE1amp (if CCNE1amp data unattainable) rebuttal

In the Figure 3 experiment, the relationship between pRPA32 and HRP is unclear and should be clarified, including the relationship with CCNE1 amplification or HRP.

-> add boxes for HR status and CCNE1amp status below waterfall plot? I.e. similar to current F1L HR status in figs

->> Andreas checks ican platform if he can see the data

Overall, the results are based on analyses of small sample sizes, with insufficient validation of validity, making interpretation of the results difficult.

-> validation of validity? What would be convincing then? I am honestly lost with this one

- highlight that our findings are confirmatory for TOPACIO and murine studies -> into discussion. We ackn that the small sample sizes are a limitation, and that the clin implications need to be validated w retrospective studies rebuttal and text

Regarding the Figure 2 experiment, it has weak connections to Figures 1 and 3, is based on analysis of small sample sizes, and has low significance of results.

The finding that combination of ATR inhibitors and ATX inhibitors activates CD8 T cells and activates CD11c-positive myeloid cells is an important observation.

-> Improve connections between figures in text. Ensure that text does not have overreaching statements in comparison to the material presented. Ensure that it is clear that this assay is done to display the potential for new therapy options for the cases that have exhausted the more standard ones.

- PoC that our model can be used in treatment-resistant cases rebuttal and text

✔️1. Line 119: Please describe in g rather than RPM as the type of centrifuge rotor is unclear.

✔️2. Line 218: Please correct the typo where "and" is italicized.

✔️3. Line 363: Please include the number as it currently shows ">XX months."

✔️4. Line 484: Please correct the typo "respondes."

5. Data availability: While WES data may be unavoidable, scRNA-seq data should be deposited.

-> scRNAseq data = everything or e.g. Cell Ranger results? Where do these go?

-JJ asks AV+Erdogan where/how and the HY/HUS contract if there are any limitations to publishing the data (we need to deposit scRNA expression matrices, can we use GEO?)

Reviewer 2

PDC response to various therapies are tested (parp, ATRi etc), but response the primary therapy – carboplatin and taxol, is not. This would add to the paper to see the response correlation with patients response.

-> apparently Ashwini had some data from responses (of these organoids?) to standard chemo (“there is a power point somewhere in P or L”)? Dig and incorporate said data to figures if it exists

- JJ asks Ashwini if this data exists. If not, we say in rebuttal that the PARPi response data should be enough and indicative

- to rebuttal Fig2 resistant patient has been tested, sensitive cannot be due to complete cell death rebuttal

The PDC response to ATRi/ATXi is interesting and an important finding with clinical implications. It would be nice to see this validated in a murine model or humanized PDX model.

-> how do we wrangle our way out of this one? Me no kill mice any more please

->> juan cubillos ruiz paper and make sure it is clearly written as already done PROBLEM SOLVED text, rebuttal

Minor –

1. For the correlation of PDC response and duration response to therapy it is unclear how many patients were tested – it would be nice to see the individual data points to know if results are skewed by the one outlier.

-> Edit figures to enhance visibility of individual datapoints, add to rebuttal this very clearly rebuttal, figs

2. How long can the immune populations remain viable in this system – studies were from 4-7 days. It would be good to know how long viable immune cells are present.

-> do we have data on this? Probably very patient-dependent, but could be added to supplement if it exists. How to characterize? Flow day 0 vs x vs y?

- JJ can ask MHO if she wants to coauth with this data that she has

—- MHO said that she does not have identical data, that Ashwini might have better suited untreated data. For Inga-Maria’s paper such data exists, day_0 have been put to the Cancer Cell paper, but the day_x would need to be analyzed but could then be used for this purpose rebuttal, figs, text; added MHO data to supplement

3. Would be nice to have some rationale for why ATRi and Wee1 inhibitors were chosen for the studies in figure 2 (there are lots of promising agents).

-> Write rationale to text (ref to ATRi trial, Wee1 trials assoc. with chemo and PARPi resistance, Anniina’s paper Cancer Res 2021(and its refs?)) rebuttal, text, injected to same part as for murine exps

4. Fig S4 A is important information but the label for the drugs used is not readable/text is too small.

-> Reformat (probably a big part of the figure) fig fully reformatted

✔️5. Line 363 – reports patient responded for ‘xx’ months – this should be corrected.

✔️6. C218_L is not explained anywhere in the text

7. In the discussion the paper mentions response to PERKi being tested, but this is not mentioned in the main results section. It is in the list of drugs tested and is in supplemental information. But if it is important enough to mention in the discussion it likely should be in the main paper.

-> PERKi data inclusion? Is in SF4A. JCR’s paper. IS SF4A LACKING SIGNIFICANCE MARKERS?

-check text, figures for PERKi results/statements and remove from discussion if fig results are ns. Rebuttal, text - removed mention

8. Im guessing this is an article formatting issue, but there is a lot of data jammed in just 3 figures. If room allows this would be better expanded into more figures and potentially including some of the supplemental information

✔️-> fig 1 into two - cut 1K,L to new fig 2, bring in SF1I,K, add data on response to Pt/tax

✔️-> split current fig 3 to two, splitting before 3D, bring back SF4A-E to new fig 4? Alternatively leave SF4A as SF, but reformat for legibility?

Figs redone

Editor comments:

The novel human ex vivo experimental system presented is timely and of interest. Results provide proof-of-principle for the potential utility of this system to investigate combinations of targeted agents with immunotherapy. Please, modify some of the the language in the abstract to emphasize the preliminary nature of the identified new combination treatment for HGSC, which requires further validation. In addition, all issues raised by the reviewers should be addressed, and some data moved from supplementary figures to main figures.

-> ensure the abstract is reformatted, the last point is fixed by revising according to reviewer comments

Joonas’ notes:

F2B: lowest boxplot colors? What are the yellow/okra colors, this patient is not treated w olaparib

-> need to check original material for figure, see which drug is in which color fixed

Manuscript length and format needs to match AACR article length limits at resubmission

-> ensure we are within the limits after revisions
